terms.definition: The rate at which a cyclist pedals, measured in revolutions per minute (RPM).
A rotational force applied to the pedals/crank that turns the drivetrain.
The ratio between chainring teeth and cassette teeth, determining pedaling effort vs speed.
Force resisting wheel motion caused by tyre deformation against the road surface.
The tendency of a spinning wheel to resist changes to its axis of rotation, aiding balance.
The point where an object's weight is evenly distributed, affecting stability.
The factor by which a mechanism multiplies input force, e.g. longer crank arms.
A defined specification (e.g. bottom bracket type) that ensures two parts fit together correctly.
